titleStmt.title: The Consul General at Shanghai ( Cabot ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Consul General at Shanghai ( Cabot ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Shanghai , April 22, 1949—7 p. m.
[Received April 22—5:45 a. m.]

div.p:
1310. Garrison Commander today informed meeting of foreign representatives attended by AMA and ANAAssistant Military and Naval Attachés, respectively. [that] women and children should be removed to safer areas since state of war now exists
in Shanghai area. He stated in his opinion major assault on Shanghai was not imminent presumably because Communists must regroup after river crossing. He emphasized however Shanghai would be defended come what might and that due to Communist infiltrations riots might break out at any moment.

Remytel 1309, April 22, 6 p. m., repeated Nanking 756, would appreciate immediate authority to publicize General Chen’s statement and to emphasize that American community should be guided accordingly.In telegram No. 717, April 22, 4 p. m., the Department replied: “Authority requested urtel 1310, April 22 granted.” If granted I shall try to act in concert with Consular colleagues.
